title: Allow user quadlets to be stored under /etc
body:
I would like to allow admin to control quadlet containers in users homedirs.

If an admin sets a quadlet in
/etc/containers/systemd/users, then all users will run these quadlet services when they login.

If an admin places a quadlet in /etc/containers/systemd/users/$(USERNAME) then only the USERNAME will execute this quadlet service when they login.

something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

```release-note
Rootless podman quadlet files can now be installed in /etc/containers/systemd/users/ directory.
```
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body:
@bachradsusi @lsm5 @ygalblum @alexlarsson @vrothberg @mheon @giuseppe PTAL

This PR will make containersh much easier to implement.

Changes LGTM, but it looks like you may have bumped up the size of quadlet too much?

```
bin/quadlet          size=  2953237  delta=218519
***************************************************************************
* bin/quadlet grew by 218519 bytes; max allowed is 51200.
*
* Please investigate, and fix if possible.
*
```

If I had podman-shell.container in /etc/containers/systemd/users, /etc/containers/systemd/users/$UID, and $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/containers/systemd, which one would be used?

I'd say it should follow from generic to users defined  - $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/containers/systemd should take a precedence over /etc/containers/systemd/users/$UID which should take precedence over /etc/containers/systemd/users

Very good question and point, @bachradsusi.

That's definitely something we should document if not done already.  Probably also emit logs when running in -drymode
Yes, but that is a question in general.  Lets merge this and then we can look into that.
